free_text: Excitation functions of some proton and deuteron
induced reactions on iron and alpha-particle induced
reactions on manganese in the energy region up to 25
MeV
decay data of the measured products
were taken from e. Browne and r.B.Firestone, table of
radioactive isotopes (V.S. Shirley, ed) wiley, new
york, 1986. The energy of the primary beam was
determined via measuring the distance between two beam
bounces according to z. Kormany, nim a337,258,1994.
the energy of the particles in each samples were
obtained from range-energy formula (C.F.Williamson et
Al.,Rapport CEA-R,3042,1966). When the covered energy
range in a foil was significant considering the
excitation functions of the investigated reactions
derived from model calculation the energy degradation
and the beam current along the stack were checked by
monitor reactions. The average beam current were
deduced from monitor reactions and from charge
collection in a faraday cup.
the primary energy was measured by time of
flight method and the uncertainty was +-0.15 Mev.
BIB section upgrated. Ref.corrected
the primary proton energy used were:
18.61 Mev and 14.87 Mev and had an uncertainty of +-
0.15 Mev. High purity Cu and Ni foils were inserted
into the stacks with different thicknesses allowing
changes in the  mean energies of the irradiated Fe
samples. Four stacks were irradiated for 20-30 min.
